headline: Legal aid reaches only 18% of eligible Malaysians
context: The Legal Aid Department served approximately 43,000 individuals in 2024, estimated to be only 18% of those financially eligible for assistance.

cards.big: 82% of eligible Malaysians cannot access legal aid due to resource gaps
cards.sub: Only 43,000 people served against an estimated 240,000 in need

cards.big: Legal Aid Act 1971 sets income threshold at RM50,000 annual household income
cards.sub: Budget allocation of RM18 million in 2025 unchanged from 2020 levels

cards.big: Article 5 of Federal Constitution guarantees right to legal counsel
cards.sub: Without accessible legal aid, constitutional right remains theoretical for many

cards.big: Rural areas have 1 legal aid lawyer per 28,000 residents on average
cards.sub: Urban-rural disparity means justice depends heavily on geography

cards.big: Access to justice is a right, not a privilege for the 18% who get through
Bar Council pro bono programme contributed 12,000 hours in 2024
